title: ranunculus prolixitorus
intro: Ranunculus prolixitorus, also known as the lesser caltha or marsh crowfoot, is a plant species that belongs to the buttercup family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being an anti-inflammatory and a natural diuretic. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing swelling and pain, treating urinary tract issues, and supporting liver function. Ranunculus prolixitorus can be prepared as an infusion or a tincture, and it is often used in herbal remedies in combination with other plants to enhance its medicinal effects.
